body.thing: an independent grader runs a builder's tests where they CANNOT reach the constrained shared resource, and the seal is measured from inside every run
body.intention: I intend that an independent grader runs a builder's tests where the builder cannot reach a constrained shared resource, and that a run whose isolation cannot be CONFIRMED is never reported as a pass. INDETERMINATE is not GREEN.

red_run.summary:
v = TesterDevice(isolation="netns").run_tests(repo=str(repo))
    
        assert v["seal_confirmed"] is True, f"the sandbox was not sealed: {v['seal_detail']}"
>       assert v["verdict"] == RED, (
            "a test that reaches out to the constrained resource must FAIL inside the sandbox — it has "
            f"no route. Got {v['verdict']} instead, which means the sandbox leaked.\n{v['tail']}"
        )

FAILED tests/tester/test_tester_isolation_is_real.py::test_a_test_run_by_the_tester_cannot_reach_the_constrained_resource
1 failed in 0.44s

body.narrative: RED at 9a925d37: the stub IS the hollow build — it ASSERTS its seal ('assumed sealed') and shells pytest on the host, so a test that opens a socket to Hex SUCCEEDS and is certified GREEN. AssertionError on verdict == RED. GREEN at d57bbd0d: NetnsIsolation wraps the run in bubblewrap --unshare-net; the seal is probed FROM INSIDE the sandbox on every run; the same test now fails with [Errno 101] Network is unreachable and earns RED. The control half is asserted too — the identical probe on the HOST must connect, or a universally-failing probe would pass for free. Not mocked: a real sandbox, a real socket, a real refusal.
body.why: Nine host-shelled pytest runs saturated Hex's single inference slot on 2026-07-13 and produced two confidently-wrong diagnoses. A policy ('run tests in series', 'don't hammer Hex') binds only the consumers who read it; --unshare-net binds all of them, including the ones nobody remembered to tell. And ContainerShim's 27 green tests mock subprocess — they have never run a real container, so its isolation guarantee is a property of an argv string, not of the world.
